subject: Computer Networking
title: Fiber Optic Cables
description: A micro-lesson that makes Fiber Optic Cables usable.

hook.text: Copper carries electricity. Fiber carries light. Light is faster, reaches farther, and ignores interference.

buildup.text: A fiber cable has a glass core thinner than a hair. Laser pulses encode data as flashes of light.

discovery.text: Single-mode fiber reaches 100+ km. Multi-mode uses multiple light paths but is limited to shorter runs.

twist.text: Fiber ignores electromagnetic interference. Run it next to power lines without issues.

climax.text: Modern fiber carries 100 Tbps per strand using wavelength-division multiplexing — many colors of light.

punchline.text: Fiber carries data as light. Nothing else matches its speed or range.

quiz.question: What advantage does fiber have over copper?
options: Immunity to electromagnetic interference and much longer range
Lower cost per meter of cable
Easier to splice and repair in the field
